name: Jerry Dodgion
abstract: Jerry Dodgion (August 29, 1932 – February 17, 2023) was an American jazz saxophonist, clarinetist and flautist. Dodgion was born in Richmond, California.

interviews.title: Jerry Dodgion interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

works.t: Concerto Grosso for Combo and Orchestra

quotes.text: Well I still have — a variety of stuff happens, it’s wonderful. I play in the Carnegie Hall band that Jon Faddis leads, and we do four concerts a year in Carnegie Hall and then we’ve been getting some more outside work. We went to Japan year before last for a week to a festival. And we went to South America this past November for ten days and we’re going to Mexico City in a couple of weeks. And we do some of the festivals in Europe in the summer, the well traveled path. And more and more stuff is coming up. And every concert is different because there’s new music written for each concert. It’s really interesting. It really keeps you on your toes. And Jon is developing into an excellent band leader now. His experience with Thad helped him quite a bit. So maybe if you get a chance you might—
quotes.caption: Jerry Dodgion discusses performing with Jon Faddis's band
quotes.doc_title: Jerry Dodgion interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Smulyan
about_quotes.evidence: And there was a band called the Philip Morris Superband... and James Moody and Herb Ellis and Urbie Green and Johnny Coles and Sweets Edison and Jerry Dodgion. I mean it was an amazing band. And we went on the road for three months.

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: I was involved with something called the American Jazz Orchestra... And this was a band comprised of mostly big band veterans: ... Jerry Dodgion
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Red Norvo
about_quotes.evidence: I called Gene Wright... He said, 'Yeah, I know, Jerry Dodgion,' so I got him and drummer and we opened in the Sands.

about_quotes.speaker: Wayne Andre
about_quotes.evidence: Phil Woods and Jerry Dodgion was playing second alto... Trombone were Jimmy Knepper, Willie Dennis and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wayne Andre interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 7, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Temperley
about_quotes.evidence: The saxophone section was Jerome Richardson and Jerry Dodgion and Joe Farrell and Eddie Daniels and myself.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Temperley interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Phil Woods
about_quotes.evidence: Jimmy Knepper and Jerry Dodgion had a room together... I was on his band in Russia in 1962
about_quotes.doc_title: Phil Woods — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
